channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Netanyahu asserts Iran's uranium enrichment and missile production severely impacted by air strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated on March 19, 2026, that Iran no longer has the capacity to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles following twenty days of U.S.-Israeli air s...

item.title: Rising Energy Prices from Iran Conflict Spark Fears of Further UK Interest Rate Increases
item.description: Energy price shocks triggered by the conflict in Iran have unsettled the UK economy, driving up inflation expectations and increasing the likelihood of further interest rate rises from the Bank of Eng...

item.title: Oil and gas prices jump as metals and global shares tumble amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Global financial markets were unsettled as a sharp rise in oil prices triggered a sell-off across metals and equities. Australian shares are expected to open lower, with ASX 200 futures down 0.5 per c...

item.title: Weekly Cryptic Crossword: JDE 522 – A South African Challenge
item.description: Mail & Guardian presents its weekly cryptic crossword, JDE 522, compiled by George Euvrard. This isn't your average puzzle – it’s proudly South African, designed specifically for a local audience and ...

item.title: Yanga held to a draw by TRA United as title race tightens
item.description: Young Africans SC, commonly known as Yanga, continued to lose ground in the Mainland Tanzania Premier League after being held to a 1–1 draw by TRA United at Sheikh Amri Abeid Stadium in Arusha on Marc...

item.title: Carpio clarifies: Only Presidents and Foreign Ministers can make binding international declarations
item.description: Former Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio clarified that only heads of state or foreign ministers can issue binding statements in international law. This statement came after the Chinese E...

item.title: A Retrospective of Dianne Wiest’s Most Memorable Film Roles
item.description: To celebrate Dianne Wiest’s 78th birthday, this article reviews twenty of her most remarkable film performances, spanning over four decades. From early supporting roles in films like 'It’s My Turn' (1...

item.title: AI-generated videos on YouTube may confuse young children and affect learning
item.description: A recent investigation by The New York Times has highlighted growing concerns over AI-generated videos targeting young children on YouTube. These short clips, often under 30 seconds, feature bizarre, ...

item.title: Pakistan Rejects Israel Ambassador's Remarks on Defence Pact and Regional Conflict
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) has strongly condemned the remarks made by Israel's ambassador to India, Reuven Azar, during an interview with Arnab Goswami, calling them a “baseless characterisation”. The FO...

item.title: Africa's Gaming Industry Sees Rapid Growth, Reaching $1.8 Billion in 2024 Driven by Mobile Platforms
item.description: Africa dey experience big big gaming revolution right now, thanks to plenty mobile connectivity, young population wey dey increase, and fast fast digital changes. The continent wey people once think s...

item.title: Pakistan Social Centre to Provide Rs1 Million Aid to Martyrs’ Families in UAE
item.description: SHARJAH – The Pakistan Social Centre in Sharjah has announced financial assistance for families of Pakistanis who lost their lives during the recent conflict in the UAE. According to Chaudhry Khalid H...

item.title: UN Secretary-General Calls for End to US-Israeli-Iranian Conflict
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has issued a strong call for an immediate de-escalation of the escalating conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. The situation has become increasin...

item.title: Canada Emphasizes Leadership Role Among Middle Powers at Global Trade Conference
item.description: At the 2026 Chatham House Global Trade Conference in London, Canada's Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand outlined the country's growing leadership role among the world’s middle powers. Anand emphasi...

item.title: Police Arrest Seven in Srinagar for Running International Cybercrime Network
item.description: According to the report from NDTV, law enforcement agencies in Srinagar have dismantled an international cybercrime syndicate operating from the region. Seven individuals have been taken into custody ...

item.title: Two teenagers killed and four injured in gang-related shooting in Elsies River
item.description: The community of Leonsdale in Elsies River, Cape Town, is in mourning after a violent shooting on Wednesday evening left two teenage boys dead and four others injured. The incident took place around 1...

item.title: Quebec Court Blocks Father and Son from Further Sperm Donations After Hundreds of Pregnancies
item.description: In a first-of-its-kind ruling in Canada, the Superior Court of Quebec has issued an injunction preventing Philippe Normand and his son Dominik Seelos from donating sperm while a trial is pending. Cour...

item.title: Dr. Jackson Omenazu Calls for Creation of Anioma State to Strengthen Igbo Unity and Correct Structural Imbalance
item.description: Dr. Omenazu Jackson, wey be Chancellor for the International Society for Social Justice and Human Rights (ISSJHR) and also one big person for Ohanaeze Ndigbo, don talk say the creation of Anioma State...

item.title: Human Activity Drives Unprecedented 20th-Century Fires in Tropical Peatlands
item.description: A recent study led by researchers from the University of Exeter reveals that wildfires in tropical peatlands have surged to levels not seen in at least 2,000 years. By analyzing charcoal preserved in ...

item.title: Apple Warns iPhone Users of Spyware Vulnerability and Advises Software Update
item.description: Apple has issued a warning to iPhone users about a newly discovered spyware called Darksword, which targets older versions of the iOS operating system released between March and August 2025 (versions ...

item.title: Pakistan Government Advises Citizens to Conserve Fuel Amid Middle East Uncertainty
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has urged citizens to save petrol and diesel to prevent possible shortages in the wake of growing instability in the Middle East. Chairing a key meeting in Islamabad, the...
